title: Present Simple Tense
content: Used for permanent situations, general truths, and habits. Structure: Subject + V.1 (s/es with he/she/it). Key markers: always, usually, often, every day.

title: Present Continuous Tense
content: Used for actions happening exactly now or around the present time. Structure: Subject + is/am/are + V-ing. Key markers: now, at the moment, currently.

title: Present Perfect Tense
content: Links past actions to the present. Used for experiences or actions with current relevance. Structure: Subject + have/has + V.3. Key markers: since, for, already, yet, just.

title: Present Perfect Continuous
content: Emphasizes the duration of an action that started in the past and continues now. Structure: Subject + have/has + been + V-ing.

title: Past Simple Tense
content: Used for completed actions at a specific time in the past. Structure: Subject + V.2 (regular -ed or irregular). Key markers: yesterday, ago, last week.

title: Past Continuous Tense
content: Used for actions that were in progress at a specific time in the past. Structure: Subject + was/were + V-ing. Often used with 'while' or 'when'.

title: Past Perfect Tense
content: The 'past of the past'. Used for an action that happened before another action in the past. Structure: Subject + had + V.3.

title: Past Perfect Continuous
content: Focuses on the duration of an action that was happening up until another point in the past. Structure: Subject + had + been + V-ing.

title: Future Simple Tense
content: Used for spontaneous decisions, predictions without evidence, or promises. Structure: Subject + will + V.inf. Alternative: 'be going to' for planned actions.

title: Future Continuous Tense
content: Used for actions that will be in progress at a specific time in the future. Structure: Subject + will + be + V-ing.

title: Future Perfect Tense
content: Used for an action that will be completed by a certain time in the future. Structure: Subject + will + have + V.3. Often uses 'by...'.

title: Future Perfect Continuous
content: Used to show how long an action will have been in progress at a point in the future. Structure: Subject + will + have + been + V-ing.

title: Passive Voice Basics
content: The focus is on the object rather than the doer. Structure: Object + be + V.3. Example: 'The cake was eaten by Mary.'

title: Passive Voice in All Tenses
content: Transformation rules from active to passive. Remember to keep the tense of 'be' consistent with the original sentence.

title: Zero Conditional
content: Used for general truths and scientific facts. Structure: If + Present Simple, Present Simple. Example: 'If you heat ice, it melts.'

title: First Conditional
content: Used for real or possible future situations. Structure: If + Present Simple, will + V.inf. Example: 'If it rains, I will stay home.'

title: Second Conditional
content: Used for hypothetical or imaginary present/future situations. Structure: If + Past Simple, would + V.inf. Example: 'If I were you, I would go.'

title: Third Conditional
content: Used for imaginary past situations (regrets). Structure: If + Past Perfect, would + have + V.3. Example: 'If I had studied, I would have passed.'

title: Mixed Conditionals
content: Combines elements of second and third conditionals to show past causes with present results or vice versa. Example: 'If I had won the lottery (past), I would be rich now (present).'

title: Vocabulary Roots: Spect, Dict, Scrib
content: Latin roots: 'Spect' (to look, e.g., inspect), 'Dict' (to say, e.g., predict), 'Scrib/Scrip' (to write, e.g., describe).

title: Greek Roots: Bio, Geo, Tele
content: Greek roots: 'Bio' (life, e.g., biology), 'Geo' (earth, e.g., geography), 'Tele' (far, e.g., telephone).

title: Common Prefixes: Un, Re, Dis
content: Prefixes change word meaning: 'Un-' (not, e.g., unhappy), 'Re-' (again, e.g., redo), 'Dis-' (opposite, e.g., disagree).

title: Common Suffixes: Tion, Ness, Ly
content: Suffixes change word class: '-Tion' (noun, e.g., action), '-Ness' (noun, e.g., kindness), '-Ly' (adverb, e.g., slowly).

title: Reading: Skimming and Scanning
content: Skimming: Reading quickly for the main idea. Scanning: Searching for specific information like names or dates.

title: Reading: Context Clues
content: Using surrounding words to guess the meaning of unknown vocabulary. Look for synonyms, antonyms, or examples.

title: Reading: Inference & Tone
content: Reading between the lines. Identifying the author's attitude (positive, negative, neutral) based on word choice.

title: Error ID: Subject-Verb Agreement
content: Singular subjects take singular verbs; plural subjects take plural verbs. Watch out for phrases between subject and verb.

title: Error ID: Parallel Structure
content: Items in a list or series must have the same grammatical form. Example: 'I like swimming, hiking, and to run' is incorrect.

title: Error ID: Pronoun Case
content: Ensure pronouns are used in the correct case (subjective vs objective). Example: 'Between you and me' (not 'you and I').

title: Error ID: Misplaced Modifiers
content: Modifiers must be placed next to the word they describe. Example: 'Walking down the street, the tree fell' implies the tree was walking.
